mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137258.0 thousand as of July 2012
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k persons in month t but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held at or near zero percent signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and supports asset valuations. The mechanism operates continuously when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of July 2012 indicates tightening bias
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of rolling 20-day windows, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 370418.0 million and HOUST at 1642.0 thousand as of July 2012
failure_condition: Joint contraction trigger fires but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profit margins and equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month decline exceeds 0.5% but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 4.8% but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11541.6 billion chained 2017 dollars as of April 2012
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% but S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of such quarters, or correlation with forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) indicates monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.8 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of June-July 2012
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint trigger fires but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,400 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential construction demand and reduced multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days in early-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1642.0 thousand as of July 2012
failure_condition: HOUST falls below 1400 thousand units but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of such instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
